preamble: The transformation of the voiceless bilabial stop /p/ into the palatal glide /j/ is a notable yet infrequent sound change. It typically occurs through a process of extreme palatalization, where the /p/ is fronted and weakened before a high front vowel like /i/ or /j/. The intermediate stages might involve a palatalized stop [pʲ] or an affricate [pç] before resolving into /j/.

examples.note: Hypothetical example of extreme yod-coalescence in rapid speech.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Wells, J. C. (1982). Accents of English.
